This morning, the operator in training was using a temper sheet that did not match the product we were running. The temper reading was -0.4, which is ideal for Almond; however, according to the sheet being used, it appeared to be in the warning range. Based on that information, the operator increased the mixer water by 0.2 in an effort to improve the temper. Shortly afterward, it was noticed that the temper sheet did not correspond to the current product.
The situation was reviewed with the operator as a training opportunity, emphasizing the importance of verifying that the temper sheet matches the product being run before making adjustments. We will continue monitoring the temper and provide additional support as needed during training.
Level 3 clean progress.

Jenny Medina Angel Cabrera Kevin Del Valle please get with the supervisors on your shifts to make sure we align on these checklists. We have our cleaning matrix, and these checklists should reflect that document, with added detail. The intent is for us to move away from using multiple checklists, and focus on one checklist to help production, and the SafetyChain check for lasting documentation. Then the matrix is the governing document.

Yes the small bars changeover was started, the parts include a vacuum section that is shorter which requires a spacer and a different sized sprocket, which equates to a longer chain. The chain that was washed will be coiled up and put in a change parts box. I'll make sure those are taken care of in the morning.
I'm not sure why the techs wouldn't have put the longer chain with narrow fingers in there already though, maybe it's in a different box.

Rough total downtime will be 2 hours and 15 mins
